failure_modes.text: Growth stalls when the step is so easy that it adds no stretch or so hard that it produces confusion, dependence, or withdrawal.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Challenge Is Mismatched to Readiness.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate the stretch: design each learning step to sit just beyond current capability — easy enough to attempt, hard enough to require genuine effort and new skill.

failure_modes.text: Experts can define the zone badly when abstraction and overconfidence hide what the novice can actually absorb next.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ground the zone in observable evidence: before setting the challenge level, assess what the learner can actually do now through demonstration, not through expert assumption.

failure_modes.text: Teams can jump into analysis too early and miss the real developmental output or current capability boundary they should be designing for.
failure_modes.mitigation: Sequence before leaping: verify that the prerequisite capabilities are in place before advancing to the next level, and diagnose whether the gap is knowledge, practice, or confidence.

premortem_questions.text: What next step is demanding enough to create growth but still reachable without confusion or shutdown?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the next level is reachable but not yet automatic.

premortem_questions.text: What outcome is actually needed here, and are we shaping support to current capability rather than to an idealized end state?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when instruction or support must match current capability, not idealized end state.

premortem_questions.text: If the current step fails, did we move on before the previous one was really complete or before the feedback loop had time to work?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Did we move on to the next step without thoroughly completing the preceding one?

heuristics.text: Calibrate challenge to what is reachable now instead of designing from the final ideal state backward with no regard for readiness.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when instruction or support must match current capability, not idealized end state.

heuristics.text: Use sequencing, feedback, and iterative progress so stretch compounds into actual learning rather than heroic failure.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when growth depends on sequencing, calibration, and feedback.

heuristics.text: Use analogies and simple explanations to make the boundary of current understanding visible before pushing the next step.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on readiness calibration, stretch-fit design, and anti-jump-the-gun structure rather than on broad developmental theory.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor stage-theory sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on calibrated next-step growth.
open_questions: The source also supports novel-problem work, but the intervention layer stays on readiness-aware support design.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on scaffolding, feynman-technique, systems-thinking, and deep-work.
antagonists: The source is strongest against status-quo comfort, authority-driven rank assumptions, and inside-view rigidity.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against status-quo-bias and authority-bias.
